summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its first meeting of 2024, administering oaths of office, electing officers, and setting the 2024 meeting schedule. The Commission then heard and approved two zoning cases: ZC 2024-001 to add eight attached single-family homes to the Lakefield Place PUD subdivision, and ZC 2024-002 to change six end units to a different model at the Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD. An upcoming zoning workshop was announced to discuss tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and a potential agricultural district.
decisions: Dave Dinwiddie elected as Vice Chair
Dave Curry to continue serving as Secretary
2024 meeting schedule approved (first Tuesday of every month)
Zoning case 2024-001 approved as recommended by staff (add 8 attached single-family homes to Lakefield Place PUD)
Zoning case 2024-002 approved as recommended by staff (change 6 end units at Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD)
Motion to adjourn at 7:05 PM approved
action_items: Zoning workshop scheduled for Monday at 6 PM to discuss tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and agricultural district
Staff to notarize and retain oath of office documents on file
key_amounts: Lakefield Place PUD: 110.20 acres total area
Lakefield Place PUD: 207 detached single-family lots plus 56 attached units for 263 total units
Attached single-family lots no less than 4,703 square feet
8.5 acres for attached single-family portion of Lakefield Place
Minimum lot width standard of 50 feet (some attached lots between 26-40 feet)
notable_topics: Oath of office for Zoning Commission members
Election of Zoning Commission officers for 2024
ZC 2024-001: Minor adjustment to Lakefield Place PUD to add 8 attached single-family homes
ZC 2024-002: Minor modification to Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD to change 6 end units to different model
Upcoming zoning workshop on tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and agricultural district
Deerfield Road traffic concerns raised by public member
notes: Transcript contains significant audio/OCR artifacts and repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting' text insertions throughout. The meeting is actually a Zoning Commission meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting, despite the repeated header text. Dan Lovis raised concerns about traffic on Deerfield Road related to the Lakefield Place development; he was told it is a county road matter. The applicant for ZC 2024-002 noted strong sales driving the requested unit changes.
title: ZC 2-6-24
